title: atriplex patula subsp. microsperma
intro: Atriplex patula subsp. microsperma, also known as a saltbush or desert holly, is a perennial shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small leaves and tiny flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat a range of health issues, including arthritis, wounds, and skin conditions, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved wound healing. Herbal preparations of Atriplex patula subsp. microsperma include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or oil.
